governing_bodies.name: Brookline Select Board
governing_bodies.note: 5-member elected board. Executive body of the town.

governing_bodies.name: Brookline Town Meeting
governing_bodies.note: UNIQUE: Representative Town Meeting with ~240 elected members from 16 precincts. This is the legislative body. Meets several times a year. Any resident can attend and speak, but only elected Town Meeting members vote. Getting elected as a TMM is relatively easy and is the most direct path to local political influence.

advocacy_orgs.name: Brookline for Everyone
advocacy_orgs.note: Active in MBTA Communities Act compliance fight

key_political_dynamics: Brookline has Town Meeting government — the most directly democratic form of local governance in the US. Historically NIMBY-leaning but shifting as younger residents push for housing. The MBTA Communities Act has been a major flashpoint — the state law requires communities near MBTA stations to zone for multifamily housing, and Brookline's compliance process has been politically contentious. Getting elected as a Town Meeting Member (TMM) is one of the highest-impact, lowest-barrier entry points to local politics anywhere in the country.
